annotations.label: Exterior algebra

anchor.snippet: the exterior algebra or Grassmann algebra of a vector space

annotations.note: `ExteriorAlgebra R M` is defined as `CliffordAlgebra (0 : QuadraticForm R M)` for a commutative semiring `R` and module `M`, which generalizes the vector-space definition.

annotations.label: Simple k-vector / blade

anchor.snippet: is called a simple

annotations.note: Searching Mathlib for `blade`, `simple k-vector`, or analogous definitions returned no result; only the image of `ιMulti` is named (not the concept of a blade).

annotations.label: k-vector and multivector

anchor.snippet: a sum of blades of homogeneous degree

annotations.note: Elements of `⋀[R]^n M` play the role of `k`-vectors, but Mathlib has no dedicated `kVector`/`multivector` definitions for the sum-of-blades vocabulary.

annotations.label: k-th exterior power

anchor.snippet: The linear span of the

annotations.note: `exteriorPower R n M` is defined as `LinearMap.range (ExteriorAlgebra.ι R) ^ n`, with notation `⋀[R]^n M`.

annotations.label: Exterior algebra as graded algebra

anchor.snippet: this makes the exterior algebra a graded algebra

annotations.note: The instance `ExteriorAlgebra.gradedAlgebra` shows that the family `⋀[R]^i M` graded-decomposes `ExteriorAlgebra R M`.

annotations.label: Universal property (informal)

anchor.snippet: The exterior algebra is universal in the sense that

annotations.note: `ExteriorAlgebra.lift` is the equiv between linear maps `M →ₗ A` whose image squares to zero and algebra maps `ExteriorAlgebra R M →ₐ A`, witnessing the universal property.

annotations.label: Area of parallelogram via determinant

anchor.snippet: The area of this parallelogram is given by the standard determinant formula

annotations.note: No Mathlib lemma states the parallelogram area = |det| in the geometric formulation; only the general `Matrix.det` is available.

annotations.label: Formal definition of exterior algebra

anchor.snippet: is defined as the quotient algebra of the tensor algebra

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `ExteriorAlgebra` as `CliffordAlgebra 0`, not as a quotient of the tensor algebra; only `TensorAlgebra.toExterior` connects the two presentations.

annotations.label: Exterior product on Lambda(V)

anchor.snippet: The exterior product

annotations.note: Multiplication in `ExteriorAlgebra R M` is the wedge product, with `ExteriorAlgebra.ιMulti` packaging the multilinear wedge of `ι R`-images as an alternating map.

annotations.label: Alternating property

anchor.snippet: The exterior product is by construction alternating on elements of

annotations.note: `ExteriorAlgebra.ι_sq_zero` states `ι R m * ι R m = 0`, the alternating property on degree-1 elements.

annotations.label: Anticommutativity from alternating

anchor.snippet: It follows that the product is also anticommutative on elements of

annotations.note: `ExteriorAlgebra.ι_add_mul_swap` states `ι R x * ι R y + ι R y * ι R x = 0`, i.e. anticommutativity on `V`.

annotations.label: Permutation sign formula

anchor.snippet: is the signature of the permutation

annotations.note: `AlternatingMap.map_perm` (specialized to `ExteriorAlgebra.ιMulti`) yields `ιMulti R n (v ∘ σ) = sign σ • ιMulti R n v`.

annotations.label: Linear dependence criterion via wedge

anchor.snippet: a necessary and sufficient condition for

annotations.note: `AlternatingMap.map_linearDependent` gives one direction (linearly dependent ⇒ wedge zero); the converse over a field via `ιMulti_family_linearIndependent_field` exists but the full iff is not packaged.

annotations.label: k-th exterior power (formal)

anchor.snippet: is the vector subspace of

annotations.note: `exteriorPower R n M` is `LinearMap.range (ι R) ^ n`, the submodule of `n`-fold products in `ExteriorAlgebra R M`.

annotations.label: Decomposable k-vector (blade)

anchor.snippet: is said to be decomposable

annotations.note: No predicate `IsDecomposable` / `IsBlade` for elements of `⋀[R]^n M` appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Non-decomposable 2-vector

anchor.snippet: the following 2-vector is not decomposable

annotations.note: No example of a non-decomposable 2-vector is recorded in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Basis of the k-th exterior power

anchor.snippet: is a basis for

annotations.note: `Module.Basis.exteriorPower` builds the basis of `⋀[R]^n M` indexed by `powersetCard I n` from a basis of `M`.

annotations.label: Dimension of k-th exterior power

anchor.snippet: the dimension of

annotations.note: `exteriorPower.finrank_eq` proves `finrank R (⋀[R]^n M) = Nat.choose (finrank R M) n` for finite free `M`.

annotations.label: Direct sum decomposition of exterior algebra

anchor.snippet: as a vector space the exterior algebra is a direct sum

annotations.note: The `DirectSum.Decomposition (fun n ↦ ⋀[R]^n M)` instance gives `ExteriorAlgebra R M ≃ ⨁ n, ⋀[R]^n M` as `R`-modules.

annotations.label: Rank of a k-vector

anchor.snippet: The rank of the k -vector

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of `rank` for an element of `⋀[R]^k M`.

annotations.label: Rank of 2-vector via coefficient matrix

anchor.snippet: The rank of a 2-vector

annotations.note: No characterization of the rank of a 2-vector through its coefficient matrix is recorded.

annotations.label: Rank-p characterization in characteristic 0

anchor.snippet: In characteristic 0, the 2-vector

annotations.note: No analogous theorem on 2-vector rank via `α^(p+1) = 0` is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Graded algebra structure

anchor.snippet: gives the exterior algebra the additional structure of a graded algebra

annotations.note: Same `ExteriorAlgebra.gradedAlgebra` instance witnesses the `ℕ`-grading by powers of `LinearMap.range (ι R)`.

annotations.label: Graded anticommutativity

anchor.snippet: The exterior product is graded anticommutative

annotations.note: The identity `α ∧ β = (-1)^{pq} β ∧ α` for homogeneous `α, β` is not recorded as a lemma in Mathlib (only the degree-1 case `ι_add_mul_swap` is).

annotations.label: Universal property of exterior algebra

anchor.snippet: the exterior algebra has the following universal property

annotations.note: `ExteriorAlgebra.lift`, with companion `ι_comp_lift` and `lift_unique`, gives the universal property statement and its uniqueness.

annotations.label: Exterior algebra of a module

anchor.snippet: we can define the exterior algebra

annotations.note: `ExteriorAlgebra R M` is defined for any commutative (semi)ring `R` and `R`-module `M`.

annotations.label: Identification with antisymmetric tensors

anchor.snippet: can be canonically identified with the vector subspace of

annotations.note: `exteriorPower.toTensorPower` provides the canonical map `⋀[R]^n M → ⨂[R]^n M` via alternatization, but no theorem identifies the image with antisymmetric tensors as a subspace.

annotations.label: Antisymmetrization of a decomposable tensor

anchor.snippet: The antisymmetrization (or sometimes the skew-symmetrization ) of a decomposable tensor is defined by

annotations.note: `MultilinearMap.alternatization` sends a multilinear map to `∑ σ, sign σ • m.domDomCongr σ`, which is the skew-symmetrization formula.

annotations.label: Isomorphism via antisymmetrization (char 0)

anchor.snippet: Assuming

annotations.note: `AlternatingMap.coe_alternatization` shows alternatization of an alternating map is `n!` times itself, the key ingredient, but the resulting characteristic-0 isomorphism `⋀^n M ≅ Λ^n M ⊆ ⨂^n M` is not packaged.

annotations.label: Alternating tensor in index notation

anchor.snippet: any alternating tensor

annotations.note: Mathlib does not use the antisymmetric component-index notation for alternating tensors.

anchor.snippet: The exterior product of two alternating tensors

annotations.note: No index-notation formula for the wedge product is stated in Mathlib.

annotations.note: The interior product itself is not defined on `ExteriorAlgebra` in Mathlib; no index-notation lemma exists.

anchor.snippet: an alternating operator from

annotations.note: `AlternatingMap R M N ι` (notation `M [⋀^ι]→ₗ[R] N`) is the bundled type of multilinear maps vanishing on inputs with two equal coordinates.

annotations.label: Universal property of alternating operators

anchor.snippet: this map is the "most general" alternating operator defined on

annotations.note: `exteriorPower.alternatingMapLinearEquiv` gives `(M [⋀^Fin n]→ₗ[R] N) ≃ₗ[R] (⋀[R]^n M →ₗ[R] N)`, the universal property of the `n`-th exterior power.

annotations.label: Alternating multilinear form

anchor.snippet: is called an alternating multilinear form

annotations.note: An alternating form is `AlternatingMap R M R ι`, the codomain-`R` specialization of `AlternatingMap`.

annotations.label: Alternating forms iso with dual exterior power

anchor.snippet: the space of alternating forms of degree

annotations.note: Specializing `alternatingMapLinearEquiv` to `N = R` gives the iso `(M [⋀^Fin n]→ₗ[R] R) ≃ₗ Module.Dual R (⋀[R]^n M)`, but no lemma names this as the dual-exterior-power identification.

annotations.label: Alternation Alt of a multilinear map

anchor.snippet: the alternation Alt of a multilinear map is defined to be

annotations.note: `MultilinearMap.alternatization` is the `Alt` operator (up to the conventional `1/k!` factor that requires characteristic zero).

annotations.label: Interior product (antiderivation)

anchor.snippet: it is possible to define an antiderivation on the algebra

annotations.note: No interior product / contraction on `ExteriorAlgebra` is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Axiomatic properties of interior product

anchor.snippet: The interior product satisfies the following properties

annotations.note: Without an interior product definition, its graded-Leibniz axiomatization is not stated.

annotations.label: Hodge duality isomorphism

anchor.snippet: the interior product induces a canonical isomorphism of vector spaces

annotations.note: No Hodge duality / Hodge star theorem is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Volume / orientation form

anchor.snippet: is sometimes called a volume form

annotations.note: No `volumeForm` definition on the top exterior power appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hodge star operator

anchor.snippet: the resulting isomorphism is called the Hodge star operator

annotations.note: No `hodgeStar` declaration exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hodge star squared as identity multiple

anchor.snippet: is always a scalar multiple of the identity map

annotations.note: Without a Hodge star, the `⋆∘⋆ = ±id` identity is absent.

annotations.label: Inner product on exterior algebra

anchor.snippet: the determinant of the matrix of inner products

annotations.note: No induced inner product on `⋀[R]^k M` (Gram-determinant formula) is defined.

annotations.label: Orthonormal basis of exterior power

anchor.snippet: constitute an orthonormal basis for

annotations.note: No statement that wedges of an ONB give an ONB of `⋀^k M` is recorded.

annotations.label: Adjointness of exterior and interior product

anchor.snippet: exterior multiplication and the interior product are mutually adjoint

annotations.note: Without an interior product, the adjointness `⟨α∧β,γ⟩ = ⟨β,ι_α γ⟩` is not formalized.

annotations.label: Bialgebra/Hopf structure on exterior algebra

anchor.snippet: inherits a bialgebra structure, and, indeed, a Hopf algebra structure

annotations.note: No `Bialgebra` / `HopfAlgebra` instance on `ExteriorAlgebra R M` is provided in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Coproduct on exterior algebra

anchor.snippet: The coproduct is a linear function

annotations.note: The shuffle-style coproduct `Δ` on `ExteriorAlgebra R M` is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Counit of exterior algebra

anchor.snippet: The counit is the homomorphism

annotations.note: `ExteriorAlgebra.algebraMapInv : ExteriorAlgebra R M →ₐ R` is the natural augmentation that would play the role of the counit, though it is not labeled as such.

annotations.label: Antipode making exterior algebra a Hopf algebra

anchor.snippet: With an antipode defined on homogeneous elements by

annotations.note: No antipode for the exterior Hopf algebra is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Functoriality of exterior algebra

anchor.snippet: there exists a unique homomorphism of graded algebras

annotations.note: `ExteriorAlgebra.map` lifts a linear map `M →ₗ N` to an algebra map of exterior algebras, with `map_id` and `map_comp_map` showing functoriality.

annotations.label: Top exterior power gives determinant

anchor.snippet: is a mapping of a one-dimensional vector space

annotations.note: Although `exteriorPower.map` and `finrank_eq` exist, no lemma identifies the induced scalar on the top exterior power with `LinearMap.det`.

annotations.label: Exactness of induced exterior sequence

anchor.snippet: is an exact sequence of graded vector spaces

annotations.note: `exteriorPower.map_surjective` proves preservation of surjectivity (and `map_injective_field` covers injectivity), but the full short-exact-sequence statement is not formalized.

annotations.label: Exterior algebra of a direct sum

anchor.snippet: the exterior algebra of a direct sum is isomorphic to the tensor product of the exterior algebras

annotations.note: No isomorphism `ExteriorAlgebra R (M ⊕ N) ≃ ExteriorAlgebra R M ⊗ ExteriorAlgebra R N` is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Natural filtration from short exact sequence

anchor.snippet: there is a natural filtration

annotations.note: No filtration on `⋀^k(M ⊕ N) = ⊕_{p+q=k} ⋀^p M ⊗ ⋀^q N` is recorded.

annotations.label: Oriented k-dimensional volume in affine space

anchor.snippet: we can define its oriented

annotations.note: No definition of an oriented `k`-volume of a `k`-simplex via the wedge product appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Boundary simplex volumes sum to zero

anchor.snippet: The sum of the

annotations.note: The boundary-volume identity for an affine simplex is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Determinant via exterior product of columns

anchor.snippet: the determinant can be defined in terms of the exterior product of the column vectors

annotations.note: No lemma stating `det A · (e₁ ∧ … ∧ eₙ) = (A e₁) ∧ … ∧ (A eₙ)` exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Electromagnetic field as differential 2-form

anchor.snippet: the electromagnetic field is generally given as a differential 2-form

annotations.note: No electromagnetism / Maxwell-form material is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geometric interpretation of decomposable k-vectors

anchor.snippet: The decomposable k -vectors have geometric interpretations

annotations.note: No correspondence between decomposable `k`-vectors and `k`-dimensional subspaces is recorded.

annotations.label: Plücker embedding of Grassmannian

anchor.snippet: This is called the Plücker embedding

annotations.note: `Module.Grassmannian` exists (as quotient parametrization) but no Plücker embedding into `ℙ(⋀^k V)` is formalized.

annotations.label: Differential forms via exterior algebra

anchor.snippet: where it is used to define differential forms

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.DifferentialForm.VectorField` but no general definition of differential forms as sections of the exterior power of the cotangent bundle.

annotations.label: de Rham cohomology from exterior algebra

anchor.snippet: is a cochain complex whose cohomology is called the de Rham cohomology

annotations.note: Mathlib's only `DeRham` file (`BDeRham`) is `p`-adic Hodge theory; classical de Rham cohomology via differential forms is not formalized.

annotations.note: No Chevalley–Eilenberg complex on `⋀ 𝔤` is defined in Mathlib.
